page: OVHcloud h100-380 is a GPU-accelerated server designed for intensive computational tasks. It features an AMD EPYC 9354 processor with 30 dedicated physical cores and 30 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 3.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 380.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.67 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the server is equipped with a single NVIDIA H100 GPU based on the Hopper architecture, which provides 80 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of 4040 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in specific workloads, particularly in large language model (LLM) inference. Prompt processing and text generation speeds across small, medium, and large models consistently rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng also reaches the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance, cached memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations fall within the top 25%. However, a notable hardware tradeoff is observed in uncached memory bandwidth for large blocks, where both read and write speeds rank in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server represents a high-density resource allocation where the integration of a premium Hopper-architecture GPU and substantial NVMe storage is balanced against the 30-core CPU profile. This configuration is optimized for workloads that heavily leverage GPU acceleration and local storage speed rather than massive CPU core counts. Appropriate workloads include LLM inference, machine learning model training, data science pipelines, and complex cryptographic processing.
description: OVHcloud h100-380 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring an AMD EPYC 9354 processor with 30 dedicated physical cores, 380.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA H100 GPU with 80 GB of VRAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based instance integrates 4040 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and an 8 Gbps network interface. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier capabilities in LLM inference and single-core CPU execution, though uncached memory bandwidth for large blocks represents a relative bottleneck. The server delivers high resource density for workloads requiring massive parallel processing and fast local storage. It is engineered for machine learning, large language model inference, data science, and cryptographic operations.
og_description: OVHcloud h100-380 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 30 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 380 GB RAM, a 4040 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA H100 GPU with 80 GB VRAM, optimized for top-tier LLM inference.
meta_description: OVHcloud h100-380 is a GPU-accelerated server with 30 AMD EPYC cores, 380 GB RAM, a 4040 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA H100 GPU with 80 GB VRAM.
tagline: A dedicated GPU server combining AMD EPYC processing, local NVMe storage, and NVIDIA H100 acceleration for demanding machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA H100 GPU with 80 GB VRAM for parallel acceleration
30 dedicated AMD EPYC 9354 cores at 3.0 GHz
380 GB system memory and 4040 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and prompt processing
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
